title: Display Asset In Maps
content:
## GPS Coordinates The Kelvin UI can display the Assets on the global map in the **Operations Overview** page. To do this go to the parent Asset and add child Assets by using the `longitude` and `latitude` keys in the array under the `properties` key of the Asset. The examples here are for creating an Asset. You can also add these `properties` when updating an Asset.
=== "Kelvin UI" You can setup the Asset's location and Relationships when creating a new Asset. You can read more on [how to create an Asset here](./create-asset.md). === "Kelvin API" Adding properties will not work in the built-in Swagger tool provided by Kelvin. If you need to add properties, then you will need to use a different API client or use curl. ```bash title="API cURL Example" linenums="1" curl -X "POST" \ "https:///api/v4/assets/create" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -H "Accept: application/json" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "name": "doc_demo_asset", "title": "Documentation Demo Asset", "asset_type_name": "pcp", "properties": [ { "name": "longitude", "title": "Longitude", "value": 37.78 }, { "name": "latitude", "title": "Latitude", "value": -122.45 } ] }' ``` The response will look like this; ```json { "name":"doc_demo_asset", "title":"Documentation Demo Asset", "status":{ "state":"unknown", "last_seen":null }, "asset_type_name":"pcp", "asset_type_title":"Doc Demo Asset", "properties":[ { "name": "longitude", "title": "Longitude", "value": 37.78 }, { "name": "latitude", "title": "Latitude", "value": -122.45 } ], "created":"2024-04-27T04:00:32.747852Z", "updated":"2024-04-27T04:00:32.747852Z" } ``` === "Kelvin API Client (Python)" ```python title="API Client (Python) Example" linenums="1" from kelvin.api.client import Client # Login client = Client(url="https://", username="") client.login(password="") # Create Asset response = client.asset.create_asset( data={ "name": "doc_demo_asset", "title": "Documentation Demo Asset", "asset_type_name": "pcp", "properties": [ { "name": "longitude", "title": "Longitude", "value": 37.78 }, { "name": "latitude", "title": "Latitude", "value": -122.45 } ] } ) print(response) ``` You will get a response similar to this; ``` title="API Client (Python) Example Response" linenums="1" asset_type_name='pcp' asset_type_title='Progressing Cavity Pump' created=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc) name='doc_demo_asset' properties=[AssetProperty(name='longitude', title='Longitude', value='37.78'), AssetProperty(name='latitude', title='Latitude', value='-122.45')] status=AssetStatusItem(last_seen=None, state=) title='Documentation Demo Asset' updated=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc) ```
## Asset Relationships The Kelvin UI can visualize relationships between Assets by connecting them with a line on the global map in the **Operations Overview** page. To do this go to the parent Asset and add child Assets by using the `associated_assets` keys in the array under the `properties` key of the Asset. The examples here are for creating an Asset. You can also add these `properties` when updating an Asset.
=== "Kelvin UI" You can setup the Asset's Relationships when creating a new Asset. You can read more on [how to create an Asset here](./create-asset.md). === "Kelvin API" Adding properties will not work in the built-in Swagger tool provided by Kelvin. If you need to add properties, then you will need to use a different API client or use curl. ```bash title="API cURL Example" linenums="1" curl -X "POST" \ "https:///api/v4/assets/create" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -H "Accept: application/json" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "name": "doc_demo_asset", "title": "Documentation Demo Asset", "asset_type_name": "pcp", "properties": [ { "name": "associated_assets", "title": "Associated Assets", "value": "plunger_lift_02,plunger_lift_03,plunger_lift_05" } ] }' ``` The response will look like this; ```json title="API cURL Example Response" linenums="1" { "name":"doc_demo_asset", "title":"Documentation Demo Asset", "status":{ "state":"unknown", "last_seen":null }, "asset_type_name":"pcp", "asset_type_title":"Doc Demo Asset", "properties":[ { "name": "associated_assets", "title": "Associated Assets", "value": "plunger_lift_02,plunger_lift_03,plunger_lift_05" } ], "created":"2024-04-27T04:00:32.747852Z", "updated":"2024-04-27T04:00:32.747852Z" } ``` === "Kelvin API Client (Python)" ```python title="API Client (Python) Example" linenums="1" from kelvin.api.client import Client # Login client = Client(url="https://", username="") client.login(password="") # Create Asset response = client.asset.create_asset( data={ "name": "doc_demo_asset", "title": "Documentation Demo Asset", "asset_type_name": "pcp", "properties": [ { "name": "associated_assets", "title": "Associated Assets", "value": "plunger_lift_02,plunger_lift_03,plunger_lift_05" } ] } ) print(response) ``` You will get a response similar to this; ```bash title="API Client (Python) Example Response" linenums="1" asset_type_name='pcp' asset_type_title='Progressing Cavity Pump' created=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc) name='doc_demo_asset' properties=[AssetProperty(name='associated_assets', title='Associated Assets', value='plunger_lift_02,plunger_lift_03,plunger_lift_05')] status=AssetStatusItem(last_seen=None, state=) title='Documentation Demo Asset' updated=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc) ```

code_examples.title: API cURL Example
API Client (Python) Example

code_examples.code: asset_type_name='pcp' asset_type_title='Progressing Cavity Pump' created=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc) name='doc_demo_asset' properties=[AssetProperty(name='longitude', title='Longitude', value='37.78'), AssetProperty(name='latitude', title='Latitude', value='-122.45')] status=AssetStatusItem(last_seen=None, state=<AssetState.unknown: 'unknown'>) title='Documentation Demo Asset' updated=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example Response

code_examples.code:
curl -X "POST" \
    "https://<url.kelvin.ai>/api/v4/assets/create" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer <Your Current Token>" \
    -H "Accept: application/json" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
            "name": "doc_demo_asset",
            "title": "Documentation Demo Asset",
            "asset_type_name": "pcp",
            "properties": [
                    {
                        "name": "associated_assets",
                        "title": "Associated Assets",
                        "value": "plunger_lift_02,plunger_lift_03,plunger_lift_05"
                    }
            ]
        }'
code_examples.title: API cURL Example
API Client (Python) Example

code_examples.code: asset_type_name='pcp' asset_type_title='Progressing Cavity Pump' created=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc) name='doc_demo_asset' properties=[AssetProperty(name='associated_assets', title='Associated Assets', value='plunger_lift_02,plunger_lift_03,plunger_lift_05')] status=AssetStatusItem(last_seen=None, state=<AssetState.unknown: 'unknown'>) title='Documentation Demo Asset' updated=datetime.datetime(2024, 5, 23, 8, 52, 58, 422300, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example Response
